checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully represents the text elements found in the source PDF, including the title, subtitle, effective date, and the list of Workforce Development Boards from the map legend. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were found.
The source document does not contain contact information such as phone numbers or email addresses, and none have been fabricated in the output JSON.
All main components of the single-page source document are present in the output. This includes the main titles, the date, the image of the map, and the 20 regional names listed in the legend.
There are no tables in the source document or the converted output.

While the county names embedded within the map image are not extracted as separate text elements, this is expected behavior for an image-based map conversion where the primary text data is the legend and header information.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the text in the source exactly in terms of content and meaning. Formatting changes like the use of a list for the legend names are acceptable per the instructions.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the source PDF and the converted JSON output. The text 'NORTH CAROLINA WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT BOARDS', 'Local Areas', 'Effective: July 1, 2023', and the 20 local area names appear exactly once in the source and once in the structural elements of the JSON. No duplication of content blocks is present.

The document structure in the JSON starts with a section title 'NORTH CAROLINA WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT BOARDS' (effectively H1) followed by an H2 'Local Areas'. There are no further headings. The hierarchy is clean and logical without any skipped levels.
The source document does not contain any tables. The legend at the bottom is correctly identified as a list rather than a table.

The reading order in the JSON follows a logical top-to-bottom sequence: Title, Heading, Metadata (Effective date), Image (Map), and List (Legend). Although the 'Effective date' text is located at the bottom left of the original page, moving it to the top after the headings is an acceptable change for accessibility to provide context early. The overall flow faithfully represents the source's organization.

The map legend, which contains 20 items in a grid layout in the source, has been correctly converted into a flat unordered list in the JSON. The order of items in the list follows a column-major reading of the legend (starting with Cape Fear and ending with Western Piedmont), which accurately captures all intended labels.
There are no hyperlinks or URLs present in the source PDF, and none have been incorrectly injected into the JSON output.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF features a color-coded legend organized in a 4-column grid that allows users to map colors on the North Carolina map to specific Workforce Development Boards. The HTML output collapses this into a single-column bulleted list and removes the color swatches entirely. This is a major visual organization failure because the functional relationship between the map colors and the labels is severed, making the map's visual organization uninterpretable for a sighted user and failing to represent the visual organization of the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: A 4-column grid legend at the bottom of the page where each name (e.g., 'Cape Fear', 'Durham') is preceded by a color-coded box corresponding to regions on the map.
checks.b_evidence_output: A single-column bulleted list of names ('• Cape Fear', '• Capital Area', etc.) with no color swatches or indicators linking them back to the map colors.
checks.b_evidence_location: Legend section, following the map image.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; the legend is a grid layout of text and swatches, and the map is a graphic. No table structures were identified in the source or the output.

Meaningful images/icons are missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the 20 color-coded swatches in the legend, which are essential for interpreting the document's content (mapping colors to board names), have been omitted. Additionally, the 'NC works' logo located in the bottom right corner of the source PDF is missing from the rendered HTML screenshot.
checks.b_evidence_source: 20 color-coded square swatches in the legend area; 'NC works' logo in the bottom right corner of the page.
checks.b_evidence_output: The legend list consists of plain text bullets only; there is no 'NC works' logo at the bottom of the page or in any other location.
checks.b_evidence_location: Legend area and footer area of the document.

checks.a_analysis: No body content, headings, or images were found to be duplicated in the HTML rendering. The document title and headers appear once in the main body.
